evals.prompt: Before I push these 15 commits to the colleague's branch, I need to ensure the entire codebase is stable. Can you test codebase integrity for me?
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive validation report with all 10 tests, showing pass/fail status, timing, and GO/NO-GO decision for sync

assertions.name: outputs_structured_report
assertions.description: Output contains clear test results table with pass/fail for each of 10 tests

assertions.name: includes_timing
assertions.description: Output shows total execution time and time-per-test

assertions.name: includes_decision
assertions.description: Output includes explicit GO/NO-GO decision ('ready to sync' or 'fix issues before pushing')

evals.prompt: I just refactored the agent import structure. Can you test just the agents module to make sure I didn't break anything?
evals.expected_output: Agent-specific test results (tests 1, 3, 10) with import validation and remediation steps if failures found

assertions.name: filters_to_agents
assertions.description: Output focuses on agent-related tests, not forecasting/metrics tests

assertions.name: includes_remediation
assertions.description: If any agent test fails, output provides specific remediation steps

evals.prompt: I changed the feature engineering logic for lag_12 and price_per_unit. Validate the forecasting module please.
evals.expected_output: Forecasting-specific tests (6, 7, 8) showing feature engineering, metrics, and numerical stability validation

assertions.name: targets_forecasting
assertions.description: Output shows only forecasting module tests, skipping agent and coordination tests

assertions.name: includes_feature_checks
assertions.description: Output explicitly validates lag_12 and price_per_unit computation
